Land Rover Range Rover Sport D250 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Range Rover Sport D250 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Range Rover Sport D250 (AJ20D6) engine typically costs £4,500–£6,500 supply only. Fitting adds £2,000–£3,000 depending on location and workshop rates. Used engines start from £2,800–£4,500 supply only. Main dealer new engines start from £10,000–£15,000+ supply only.

mainEntity.name: Is the Range Rover Sport D250 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ20D6 is a 3.0-litre six-cylinder Ingenium diesel introduced in the Range Rover Sport from July 2020. It's generally considered more reliable than the 2.0-litre four-cylinder Ingenium diesel, with a good service life (250,000+ km). However, DPF clogging in urban use, timing chain tensioner wear, and EGR cooler issues are well-documented patterns across the Ingenium diesel family.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover Sport D250?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) DPF clogging — particularly on vehicles used for short urban journeys, (2) Timing chain tensioner wear — a known pattern across Ingenium diesels, and (3) EGR cooler coolant leaks — a vulnerability across Land Rover's diesel engine range.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Sport D250 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £25,000–£45,000 Range Rover Sport D250, a £4,500–£6,500 recon is 10–25% of the car's value — worth it given Range Rover Sport values have remained relatively strong. However, for higher-mileage examples, evaluate the vehicle's overall condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Range Rover Sport D250 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The D250 uses the AJ20D6 (also known as DT306) — a 3.0-litre six-cylinder diesel producing 249 PS and 600 Nm of torque. The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent timing chain failure on the D250?

acceptedAnswer.text: Prevention is not guaranteed, but you can significantly reduce the risk: (1) Change oil every 10,000–12,000 miles with the correct JLR-spec oil, (2) Avoid short urban journeys — regular motorway runs allow DPF regeneration to complete, and (3) Never ignore cold-start rattle — book an inspection immediately if you hear it. Even with perfect maintenance, timing chain tensioner wear remains a risk on higher-mileage examples.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Range Rover Sport D250 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current L494/L461-generation AJ20D6, the only engine used in the Range Rover Sport D250.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Range Rover Sport D250 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

problems.title: Diesel Particulate Filter (DPF) Clogging
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, reduced performance, increased fuel consumption, "Restricted Performance" message on instrument cluster, engine warning light
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — short urban journeys accelerate the issue
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if warning appears

problems.recommendation: Like all modern diesels, the D250's 3.0-litre Ingenium diesel is sensitive to DPF clogging if used predominantly for short urban trips. The vehicle's 48V mild-hybrid system helps, but it doesn't eliminate the need for regular motorway running to complete passive DPF regenerations. If you're buying a D250 primarily for city use, factor in occasional longer runs or consider the P400 petrol instead. If the DPF warning appears, book a forced regeneration immediately — ignoring it can lead to expensive DPF replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full DPF clogging guide

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.symptoms: Cold-start rattling lasting 1–30 seconds, metallic ticking from engine, engine warning light, rough running

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The AJ20D6 shares the Ingenium diesel family's timing chain design. While the six-cylinder unit is more robust than the 2.0-litre four-cylinder, timing chain tensioner wear remains a documented pattern across the Ingenium diesel range. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain inspected — a £1,500–£3,000 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £6,500+ replacement that follows a snapped chain. The chain is rear-mounted on many Ingenium diesel engines, significantly increasing labour costs.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: EGR Cooler Coolant Leak
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white exhaust smoke, occasional burning smell, engine overheating
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — a known vulnerability across Land Rover diesel engines
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not typically progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant level drops frequently

problems.recommendation: The EGR cooler is a known vulnerability across Land Rover's diesel engine range. Internal cooler leaks can cause coolant contamination and, in extreme cases, hydro-lock and catastrophic engine damage. If you're topping up coolant regularly without visible external leaks, book a pressure test immediately. A £750–£1,100 EGR cooler replacement is far cheaper than the engine damage that follows if ignored.

failureLink.label: Read the full EGR cooler failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover Sport D250 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Full engine replacement is the only reliable fix

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover Sport D250? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with 10,000–12,000-mile oil change intervals — the Ingenium is unforgiving of neglect
* Evidence of regular motorway use (DPF health)
* No cold-start rattle — this is a critical warning sign
* Remaining manufacturer warranty — these are complex vehicles
avoidIf: * Any cold-start rattle — this is a warning sign of timing chain tensioner wear
* DPF warning lights or "Restricted Performance" messages
* Coolant loss with no documented fix — could indicate EGR cooler failure
* No service history or extended oil change intervals — the Ingenium diesel family is unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Range Rover Sport D250's AJ20D6 is a modern, sophisticated 3.0-litre six-cylinder diesel that represents a meaningful step up from the four-cylinder Ingenium units in terms of refinement and reliability. The engine has a good service life (250,000+ km), but DPF clogging in urban use, timing chain tensioner wear, and EGR cooler issues remain well-documented patterns across the Ingenium diesel family. Buy with full service history, evidence of regular motorway use, and budget for preventative maintenance. Without history? Approach with caution — the repair bills can be substantial.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover Sport D250 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Sport D250 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking from the engine (timing chain tensioner warning — this is critical)
* DPF History — Ask about driving patterns; short urban journeys are a red flag
* Service History — Including the AJ20D6 engine and confirmed 10,000–12,000-mile oil change intervals — non-negotiable
* Coolant Level — Check for unexplained loss (EGR cooler leak)
* Restricted Performance — Any warning messages? This often indicates DPF or turbo issues
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup (EGR cooler or injector issues)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* Warranty Status — Manufacturer warranty is valuable on these complex vehicles
